Two questions ordinary (mean) regression answer: household income protect crime equally across distribution, matter high-crime neighbourhoods (upper tail)? income housing value accounted , neighbouring areas still correlated crime, spatial spillover model missing, can absorb ? spatial-error (CAR) quantile regression spmixqr answers . fits quantile regression level tau adds spatial random effect, built neighbourhood weights matrix, soaks residual spatial dependence.

title: A primer on spatial quantile regression with autocorrelation
text: chosen quantile level τ\tau, τ\tau-th conditional quantile outcome region ss Qτ(ys∣xs)=xs′β+ϕs,ϕ∼CAR(W,α,λϕ). Q_\tau(y_s \mid x_s) = x_s'\beta + \phi_s, \qquad \phi \sim \text{CAR}(W,\alpha,\lambda_\phi).  β\beta covariate effects quantile τ\tau, ϕ\phi spatial random effect, one value per region, conditional-autoregressive precision built spatial weights matrix WW. use Leroux form Q(α)=α(D−W)+(1−α)IQ(\alpha)=\alpha(D-W)+(1-\alpha), DD degree matrix: α\alpha controls spatial strength λϕ\lambda_\phi smoothness. constrain ϕ\phi sum zero within connected component, intercept carries overall level ϕ\phi mean-zero spatial deviation. quantile fit uses asymmetric-Laplace (check-loss) working likelihood; estimation penalised EM CAR precision enters sparse ridge penalty (Kozumi Kobayashi 2011; Leroux, Lei, Breslow 2000). one regime (G = 1, Example ) model single-population spatial-error quantile regression; G > 1 (Example B) becomes mixture quantile regressions latent regimes. full mixture spatially-varying-slope feature set vignette("spmixqr-mixtures").

title: A primer on spatial quantile regression with autocorrelation
text: model leave behind spatial structure, neighbouring areas correlated residual crime? moran_resid() computes permutation Moran’s residuals CAR term. spatial term, residuals show borderline positive clustering (Moran’s ≈0.17\approx 0.17, p≈0.05p \approx 0.05): nearby neighbourhoods similar left-crime. CAR term, clustering drops non-significant (≈0.15I \approx 0.15, p≈0.10p \approx 0.10). shift modest real: spatial effect absorbs part spillover, remains longer clears usual threshold. fitted ϕ\phi estimated spatial deviations, “residual hotspot/coldspot” surface income housing value explain. map alone enough: roughness-penalised surface always looks smooth, need know deviations reliable. phi_surface(ci = TRUE) attaches bootstrap standard errors flags credible units whose interval excludes zero.
code: mr <- moran_resid(fit, nsim = 999) mr #> Permutation Moran's I (responsibility-weighted unit residuals) #>   before CAR: I = 0.1710   p = 0.0490   (units = 49) #>   after  CAR: I = 0.1448   p = 0.0990   (units = 49) ph <- phi_surface(fit, ci = TRUE)              # adds se, lower, upper, credible head(round(ph[, c("phi", "se", "lower", "upper")], 2)) #>     phi   se lower upper #> 1 -0.25 0.08 -0.40 -0.10 #> 2 -0.16 0.09 -0.33  0.02 #> 3 -0.08 0.08 -0.24  0.08 #> 4 -0.04 0.08 -0.19  0.12 #> 5  0.30 0.04  0.22  0.39 #> 6  0.10 0.06 -0.01  0.22 sprintf("%d of %d neighbourhoods are credible hot/cold spots", sum(ph$credible), nrow(ph)) #> [1] "39 of 49 neighbourhoods are credible hot/cold spots"

title: A primer on spatial quantile regression with autocorrelation

Read credible ϕ\phi outcome’s units: value , say, +8+8 means median crime rate eight crimes per 1000 households income housing value predict, reliably . (outcome modelled log scale, phi_surface(scale = "exp") instead reports exp(phi) multiplier — 1.18 reads “18% expected” — vignette("spmixqr-spatial-error") shows.)  ϕ\phi map localises spillover Moran’s test flagged: cluster neighbourhoods whose crime sits income housing value imply, CAR term pools toward neighbours. income--tau curve puts headline finding one picture.  slope grows negative move crime distribution.
code: library(sf) shp <- system.file("shapes/columbus.gpkg", package = "spmixqr") cols <- st_read(shp, quiet = TRUE)               # 49 neighbourhood polygons st_crs(cols) <- NA                               # Columbus coords are arbitrary planar units ps         <- phi_surface(fit, ci = TRUE)        # CAR effect + uncertainty, per region cols$crime <- columbus_crime$crime cols$phi   <- ps$phi                             # the CAR spatial effect cols$credible_phi <- ifelse(ps$credible, ps$phi, NA)   # keep only reliable hot/cold spots cols$resid <- residuals(fit)                     # observed minus fitted median crime library(ggplot2) theme_set(theme_void(base_size = 11)) ggplot(cols) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = phi), colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2c7bb6", mid = "grey92", high = "#d7191c") +   labs(title = "CAR spatial effect on crime",        subtitle = "residual neighbourhood level (crimes per 1000) beyond income and housing",        fill = expression(phi)) ggplot(cols) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = credible_phi), colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2c7bb6", mid = "grey92", high = "#d7191c",                        na.value = "grey92") +   labs(title = "Credible hot/cold spots only",        subtitle = "grey = interval includes zero (not reliably different from expected)",        fill = expression(phi)) ggplot(cols) +   geom_sf(aes(fill = resid), colour = "grey40", linewidth = 0.2) +   scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2c7bb6", mid = "grey92", high = "#d7191c") +   labs(title = "Median-model residuals", fill = "resid") ggplot(data.frame(tau = taus, income = inc), aes(tau, income)) +   geom_line() + geom_point() + geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 3) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   labs(title = "Income effect on crime across the distribution",        x = expression(tau), y = "income coefficient")

what: 10. Example B: a mixture of housing submarkets
title: A primer on spatial quantile regression with autocorrelation
text: mixture needs enough data resolve distinct regimes, example turn larger dataset: roughly two thousand house sales Lucas County, Ohio (seeded subsample classic real-estate data, shipped lucas_house). question whether single price equation fits whole county, whether submarkets — groups homes whose price responds differently size age — . point observations, use point coordinates rather polygons. G = 2 mixture lets data split two latent regimes. give mixing spatial gate (spatial_gate = TRUE), probability belonging submarket smooth surface county rather constant. BIC prefers mixture decisively single equation . regimes share price–size elasticity near 0.9 (1% larger home costs 0.9% ), differ steeply price falls age: one submarket discounts age mildly (age coefficient near −0.8-0.8), steeply (near −1.7-1.7, per-century age scale data). mixture found two price processes single regression impose one. spatial gate makes probability steep-discount submarket surface. map surface, assigned submarket, residuals point geometry.   gate surface localises steep-discount submarket part county rather scattering random — mixing genuinely spatial. residual map reality check: flexible mixture can fit closely -sample, look spatial pattern left behind.  reporting, spatial-block bootstrap (variance = "boot") carries regime labels resampling; vignette("spmixqr-mixtures") covers gate slope-surface machinery full.
code: data(lucas_house) lucas <- transform(lucas_house, lprice = log(price), ltla = log(tla)) nrow(lucas) #> [1] 2000 coords <- as.matrix(lucas[, c("x", "y")]) fitm <- spmixqr(lprice ~ ltla + age, data = lucas, coords = coords,                 G = 2, tau = 0.5, spatial_gate = TRUE, spatial_coef = FALSE,                 control = spmixqr_control(nstart = 4L, k = 20L, seed = 1)) round(coef(fitm), 2)          # one column per submarket #>             regime1 regime2 #> (Intercept)    5.16    5.10 #> ltla           0.90    0.92 #> age           -0.77   -1.74 lucas_sf <- st_as_sf(lucas, coords = c("x", "y")) lucas_sf$p_steep <- gate_surface(fitm)$prob[gate_surface(fitm)$regime == "2"] lucas_sf$regime  <- factor(apply(predict(fitm, type = "posterior"), 1, which.max)) lucas_sf$resid   <- residuals(fitm) ggplot(lucas_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(colour = p_steep), size = 0.6) +   scale_colour_viridis_c(limits = c(0, 1)) +   labs(title = "Spatial gate: P(steep age-discount submarket)", colour = "prob") ggplot(lucas_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(colour = regime), size = 0.6) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   labs(title = "Assigned submarket", colour = "regime") ggplot(lucas_sf) +   geom_sf(aes(colour = resid), size = 0.6) +   scale_colour_gradient2(low = "#2c7bb6", mid = "grey85", high = "#d7191c") +   labs(title = "Mixture-model residuals", colour = "resid")

what: 13. How spmixqr relates to other tools
title: A primer on spatial quantile regression with autocorrelation
text: quantreg fits quantile regression spatial structure; spatialreg spdep fit spatial autoregressive mean models; CARBayes fits CAR models quantile likelihood. dedicated spatial quantile package, McSpatial, archived since 2021. spmixqr provides maintained spatial-error quantile regression first-class queen, rook, distance, k-NN, user-supplied weights, Moran’s-diagnostic, mixture extension (Reich, Fuentes, Dunson 2011).

what: What the CAR spatial-error term adds
title: Spatial CAR-error quantile regression (areal and point data)
text: spmixqr v0.1.0 modelled spatial structure mgcv basis gate slope surfaces. Version 0.2.0 adds weights-matrix-driven alternative: regime carries per-unit conditional-autoregressive (CAR) spatial random effect phi_k, mean-zero spatial-level surface Leroux proper precision Q(α)=α(D−W)+(1−α),α∈[0,1],Q(\alpha) = \alpha (D - W) + (1-\alpha) , \qquad \alpha \[0,1], recovers intrinsic CAR (ICAR/Besag) α=1\alpha = 1 independence α=0\alpha = 0(Leroux, Lei, Breslow 2000). asymmetric-Laplace working likelihood quantile components makes Gaussian CAR effect right penalty M-step (Kozumi Kobayashi 2011). solve penalised check loss directly, λϕ\lambda_\phi tuning parameter chosen BIC. G = 1 single-population spatial-error quantile regression; G > 1 mixture spatial quantile regressions. Identification. free spatial gate aliases CAR intercept surface, spatial_error = TRUE turns spatial gate default, explicit spatial_gate = TRUE raises guardrail error. per-connected-component sum--zero constraint phi_k absorbed incidence precision (mgcv’s mrf basis via absorb.cons), intercept carries level CAR surface mean-zero deviation. alpha fixed (default 0.95) disclosed weakly identified; check loss select (verified degenerate).

title: Spatial CAR-error quantile regression (areal and point data)

what: NNGP: a weights-matrix-free spatial error for point data
title: Spatial CAR-error quantile regression (areal and point data)
text: point data, contiguity/k-NN W ad hoc discretisation continuous surface. nearest-neighbour Gaussian process (NNGP; Datta, Banerjee, Finley & Gelfand 2016) instead places continuous Matern field points represents sparse precision Q = (- B)' D^{-1} (- B) built point’s m nearest neighbours. spq_weights(type = "nngp") returns precision; plugs penalised spatial-error M-step CAR term — W-free, scalable (precision sparse), continuous-domain. precision proper (full rank), phi fit unconstrained (sum--zero). Matern range smoothness nu weakly identified one realisation (Zhang 2004), fixed (chosen BIC spq_weights(range = ...) objects), exactly roughness penalty . point GPs use variance = "boot": classification-conditional sandwich forms dense per-unit covariance.

title: Spatial CAR-error quantile regression (areal and point data)
text: Adding spatial random effect — CAR NNGP — can bias covariate effects covariate varies smoothly space: spatial term covariate compete variation, attenuating (even flipping) slope (Hodges & Reich 2010). sound remedy Spatial+ (Dupont, Wood & Augustin 2022): residualise covariate spatial smooth fit residuals, covariate keeps non-spatial variation. loss-agnostic, composes quantile EM. (offer restricted spatial regression, recent literature refuted general fix.) sim_spatial_confound() makes problem concrete: covariate x confounded spatial field also drives y, known true slope 1. naive slope badly inflated; NNGP term absorbs part confounding , penalised, leaves residual bias; Spatial+ removes remains. catch (Frisch–Waugh–Lovell): Spatial+ deconfounds extent residualisation smooth -resolves penalised spatial term — residualising basis -op. residualisation mean-based, cleanly deconfounds quantile slope symmetric error; reported slopes effect non-spatial part covariate (see summary(f_splus)).
code: d <- sim_spatial_confound(n = 400, beta = 1.0, confound = 0.9, seed = 11) Wn2 <- spq_weights(d$coords, type = "nngp", m = 10) ctl <- spmixqr_control(nstart = 1L, seed = 1) b <- function(f) round(coef(f)["x", 1], 3) f_naive <- spmixqr(y ~ x, d$data, coords = d$coords, G = 1, tau = 0.5,                    spatial_error = FALSE, spatial_coef = FALSE, variance = "none", control = ctl) f_nngp  <- spmixqr(y ~ x, d$data, coords = d$coords, G = 1, tau = 0.5, spatial_error = TRUE,                    spatial_coef = FALSE, spatial_W = Wn2, variance = "none", control = ctl) #> spatial_error = TRUE: turning the spatial gate off (the free spatial gate aliases with the CAR intercept surface). Set gating = ~z for a covariate gate. f_splus <- spmixqr(y ~ x, d$data, coords = d$coords, G = 1, tau = 0.5, spatial_error = TRUE,                    spatial_coef = FALSE, spatial_W = Wn2, spatial_plus = TRUE,                    variance = "none", control = ctl) #> spatial_error = TRUE: turning the spatial gate off (the free spatial gate aliases with the CAR intercept surface). Set gating = ~z for a covariate gate. c(truth = 1, naive = b(f_naive), nngp_only = b(f_nngp), spatial_plus = b(f_splus)) #>        truth        naive    nngp_only spatial_plus  #>        1.000        1.979        1.258        1.087

what: Identification under the check loss (why mean-based residualisation works)
title: Spatial CAR-error quantile regression (areal and point data)
text: Spatial+ derived least-squares (mean) regression, mean-based residualisation deconfound quantile slope? Yes — every quantile . additive model y = m(s) + beta_x x + eps exogenous error eps x = E[x | s] + e, removing E[x | s] makes conditional quantile affine residual slope beta_x every tau: Q_tau(y | r, s) = beta_x r + h_tau(s), h_tau(s) absorbed retained spatial term. key point deconfounding conditional-orthogonality property — depend tau — single least-squares residualisation works across whole distribution; need residualise separately per quantile. (full proof boundary cases package’s methodological notes.) recovery holds tails, just median: two practical conditions covariate enters additively (single slope exists) residualisation smooth, together spatial term, -resolves covariate’s spatial mean (Frisch–Waugh–Lovell gap ).
code: taus <- c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9) rbind_tau <- function(tau) {   fn <- spmixqr(y ~ x, d$data, coords = d$coords, G = 1, tau = tau, spatial_error = FALSE,                 spatial_coef = FALSE, variance = "none", control = ctl)   fp <- spmixqr(y ~ x, d$data, coords = d$coords, G = 1, tau = tau, spatial_error = TRUE,                 spatial_coef = FALSE, spatial_W = Wn2, spatial_plus = TRUE,                 variance = "none", control = ctl)   c(tau = tau, naive = b(fn), spatial_plus = b(fp)) } round(do.call(rbind, lapply(taus, rbind_tau)), 3)   # true slope = 1 at every tau #> spatial_error = TRUE: turning the spatial gate off (the free spatial gate aliases with the CAR intercept surface). Set gating = ~z for a covariate gate. #> spatial_error = TRUE: turning the spatial gate off (the free spatial gate aliases with the CAR intercept surface). Set gating = ~z for a covariate gate. #> spatial_error = TRUE: turning the spatial gate off (the free spatial gate aliases with the CAR intercept surface). Set gating = ~z for a covariate gate. #>      tau naive spatial_plus #> [1,] 0.1 1.921        1.195 #> [2,] 0.5 1.979        1.087 #> [3,] 0.9 1.889        1.072

title: Validation: recovery, coverage, and negative controls
text: simulate two-regime spatial mixture, fit training set, measure recovered gate surface truth held-locations. gate surface regime membership recovered locations used fitting.
code: d <- sim_spmixqr(n = 400, G = 2, tau = 0.5, seed = 2) tr <- seq_len(300); te <- 301:400 fit <- spmixqr(y ~ x, d$data[tr, ], coords = d$coords[tr, ], G = 2, tau = 0.5,                variance = "none", control = spmixqr_control(nstart = 4L, seed = 1)) pr_te <- predict(fit, newdata = d$data[te, ], newcoords = d$coords[te, ], type = "prob") gate_cor <- cor(pr_te[, 2], d$truth$pi[te, 2]) cl <- apply(predict(fit, newdata = d$data[te, ], newcoords = d$coords[te, ],                     type = "prob"), 1, which.max) acc <- max(mean(cl == d$truth$z[te]), mean((3 - cl) == d$truth$z[te])) round(c(held_out_gate_cor = abs(gate_cor), held_out_accuracy = acc), 3) #> held_out_gate_cor held_out_accuracy  #>             0.994             0.750

title: Spatial quantile regression with autocorrelation
text: Spatial weights, way. spq_weights() builds W queen/rook contiguity (sf/sp polygons), distance bands, k-nearest neighbours, accepts user-supplied matrix. CAR spatial-error term (spatial_error = TRUE): mean-zero conditional- autoregressive surface Leroux proper-CAR / ICAR precision absorbs residual spatial autocorrelation, /Moran’s diagnostic phi_surface() map. Quantile regression tau, covariate effects can differ across distribution (tails vs centre) — single-region finite mixture spatial quantile regressions (spatially varying mixing slope surfaces). Penalised EM (sparse CAR penalty; mgcv smooths gate/slope surfaces); exact reductions quantreg, mixqr, mixqrgate. Classification-aware inference: spatial-block bootstrap (recommended) plus fast sandwich; spmixqr_select() regimes smoothing; full S3 surface, surface accessors, maps.

NULL point data. G Number regimes. tau Quantile level (0, 1). gating Gating-covariate formula (besides space), e.g. ~ z. Default ~ 1. spatial_gate Logical; let mixing probabilities vary space. spatial_coef Logical; let component slopes vary space (scalar intercepts; see Details). spatial_error Logical; add per-regime conditional-autoregressive (CAR) spatial random effect phi_k (mean-zero spatial-level surface) component. TRUE spatial gate turned default (free spatial gate aliases CAR intercept surface; see Details); explicit spatial_gate = TRUE raises guardrail error. spatial_W spq_weights() object square weights matrix CAR term. NULL auto-builds: queen contiguity areal (nb/listw), else k-nearest-neighbour graph point coords. car CAR precision family: "proper" (Leroux alpha(D-W)+(1-alpha), default) "icar" (intrinsic D-W sum--zero constraint). car_alpha Proper-CAR spatial-dependence strength [0, 1] (default 0.95). Fixed default (weakly identified; disclosed summary()), selected check loss. spatial_plus Logical; apply Spatial+ confounding safeguard (Dupont, Wood & Augustin 2022): residualise covariate spatial smooth fit residuals, smoothly-spatial covariate longer competes spatial random effect. reported slopes effect non-spatial part covariate. Deconfounds extent residualisation smooth -resolves (penalised) spatial term (Frisch–Waugh–Lovell); default smooth made richer reason. NULL uses control default; choose spmixqr_select(). variance Inference: "sandwich" (fast, classification-conditional; default), "boot" (spatial-block/xy bootstrap; recommended reporting), "none". basis Advanced override: prebuilt spmixqr_basis(). title: Construct a spatial weights object for the CAR-error term — spq_weights
text: x spatial object. type = "queen"/"rook": sf/sp polygon object, spdep nb/listw (used directly). type = "distance"/"knn": two-column coordinate matrix/data frame. type = "supplied": square nonnegative matrix (Matrix base). type Weights construction: "queen"/"rook" (polygon contiguity), "distance" (distance band [d1, d2]), "knn" (k nearest neighbours, symmetrised), "supplied" (validate user matrix), "nngp" (sparse nearest-neighbour Gaussian-process Matern precision point data: scalable, W-free continuous-domain spatial-error alternative CAR; Datta et al. 2016). d1, d2 Lower / upper distance band (required "distance"). k Number nearest neighbours ("knn"). style spdep weighting style; "B" (symmetric binary, default) required valid GMRF precision. "W" (row-standardised) asymmetric triggers warning (symmetrised use). ids Optional unit identifiers (row/column names W). m type = "nngp": number nearest earlier-neighbours (default 10). range type = "nngp": Matern range raw coordinate units (NULL uses ~0.1 domain extent). Weakly identified one realisation, fixed selected (like smoothing penalty), estimated (Zhang 2004). nu type = "nngp": Matern smoothness, 0.5 (exponential) 1.5. ... Currently unused.

spmixqr 0.3.1
text: Spatial-error vignette: added formal “Identification check loss” result Spatial+. additive (location-shift) covariate effect exogenous error, single mean-based (least-squares) covariate residualisation deconfounds covariate slope every quantile – deconfounding tau-free conditional-orthogonality property – given residualisation smooth (spatial term) -resolves covariate’s spatial mean. Demonstrated tau = 0.1, 0.5, 0.9; summary() wording updated accordingly. (code behaviour change.)

title: spmixqr 0.3.0
text: Two spatial-dependence additions alongside existing CAR-phi, grounded verified review recent (2019-2026) advances. CAR-phi kept (adequate areal data; fixed effects relatively robust weights-matrix misspecification). NNGP point-data spatial-error engine. spq_weights(type = "nngp", m, range, nu) builds sparse nearest-neighbour Gaussian-process Matern precision (Datta et al. 2016) point/distance data – scalable, weights-matrix-free, continuous-domain alternative contiguity CAR. plugs penalised spatial-error M-step; precision proper/full-rank, phi fit unconstrained (sum--zero). summary(), phi_surface(), maps, residual-Moran diagnostic, spatial-block bootstrap work NNGP fits. Spatial+ confounding safeguard. spmixqr(spatial_plus = TRUE) residualises covariate spatial smooth (Dupont, Wood & Augustin 2022) smoothly-spatial covariate longer competes spatial random effect; reported slopes effect non-spatial part covariate. Loss-agnostic, composes quantile EM CAR NNGP error. deconfounds extent residualisation smooth -resolves penalised spatial term (default smooth made rich reason). Restricted spatial regression (RSR) deliberately offered (refuted general fix). sim_spatial_confound() generates point data (without) spatial confounding demonstrations validation. spmixqr_select() threads spatial_plus (residualisation refit per CV fold, leakage).

title: spmixqr 0.2.5
text: Primer: dropped “Reporting reproducibility” section.

title: spmixqr 0.2.4
text: interpretable spatial-error (CAR) coefficients. phi_surface() gains ci = TRUE (adds bootstrap se, lower, upper, credible flag units whose interval excludes zero) scale = "exp" (adds mult = exp(phi), multiplicative deviation log-outcome models, e.g. 1.18 = “18% expected”). Standard errors propagate fit’s coefficient covariance sum--zero constraint, Var(phi) = T V T'; bootstrap recommended sandwich, ignores penalty. plot(fit, = "phi", credible = TRUE) greys units reliably different zero, mapping credible hot/cold spots. primer now reads spatial effect uncertainty (credible-hotspot map outcome-scale interpretation); spatial-error article shows exp() multiplicative reading log zinc.

title: spmixqr 0.2.3
text: Mixture example moved larger, appropriate dataset. Documentation/data . New built-dataset lucas_house: 2000-row subsample Lucas County, Ohio house sales. primer’s mixture example (Example B) now fits two-regime spatially gated mixture quantile regressions (housing submarkets discount age different rates), point maps gate-probability surface, assigned submarket, residuals. 49-neighbourhood Columbus data small resolve mixture longer used (remains Example , single-region CAR fit).

title: spmixqr 0.2.2
text: Primer expanded maps mixture example. Documentation . Columbus shapefile now ships inst/shapes/columbus.gpkg. primer reads sf::st_read() draws choropleth maps spatial-error surface residuals (replacing earlier centroid scatter), demonstrating shapefile--sf--map workflow. second worked example fits two-regime mixture (G = 2) quantile regressions Columbus crime, maps assigned regimes residuals, reports per-regime coefficient table.

title: spmixqr 0.2.1
text: Documentation refocus: spatial-dependence features (CAR spatial-error term, weights-matrix construction, Moran’s autocorrelation diagnostics) now package’s primary framing, finite-mixture features presented extension. change estimation code API. New built-dataset columbus_crime (49 Columbus, Ohio neighbourhoods) queen-contiguity weights columbus_W, rewritten primer. primer (vignette("spmixqr-primer")) rewritten fully worked spatial-error quantile regression Columbus crime: building weights, fitting, reading income effect across crime distribution, absorbing residual spatial autocorrelation (Moran’s /), mapping spatial effect, reporting. earlier mixture/gate/slope walk-moves vignette("spmixqr-mixtures"). README, DESCRIPTION, pkgdown site lead spatial-autocorrelation capabilities.

title: spmixqr 0.2.0
text: Adds conditional-autoregressive (CAR) spatial-error module. Fully back-compatible: spatial_error = FALSE (default) reproduces v0.1.0 exactly. New spatial_error = TRUE argument spmixqr(): regime carries mean-zero per-unit CAR/ICAR spatial random effect phi_k Leroux proper precision Q(alpha) = alpha (D - W) + (1 - alpha) (intrinsic D - W). per-connected- component sum--zero constraint absorbed incidence/precision (à la mgcv’s absorb.cons), intercept carries level phi_k identified. G = 1 maintained single-population spatial-error quantile regression; G > 1 (covariate/constant gate) mixture spatial quantile regressions. New weights layer spq_weights(): builds symmetric, sparse W sf/sp polygons (queen/rook contiguity), point coordinates (distance band, k-NN, symmetrised), spdep nb/listw, user matrix (supplied). make_car_precision() forms CAR/ICAR precision; print.spq_weights(). component M-step rebranched: spatial_error = TRUE routes new sparse penalised-Newton solver (Newton step solved Matrix::solve() sparse (p + L') system) augmented [X, R] design, regardless spatial_coef, CAR penalty never dropped. dense solver remains non-CAR path. Identification guardrail: spatial_error = TRUE turns spatial gate default (message); explicit spatial_gate = TRUE alongside spatial_error = TRUE raises explanatory error (free spatial gate aliases CAR level surface). moran_resid(): permutation Moran’s responsibility-weighted residual aggregated spatial unit, reported /CAR term. phi_surface() tidy accessor; plot(= "phi"); spatial-error block summary(). alpha fixed (default proper-CAR 0.95, disclosed weakly identified, selected check loss); lambda_error (= lambda_phi) selected BIC CAR effective-df term. spmixqr_select() gains lambda_error/car/car_alpha pass-. Inference: bootstrap_vcov() gains areal connected-component block mode (spdep::n.comp.nb) CAR fits (point path silently fell back ..d.). sim_spmixqr(spatial_error = TRUE) generates lattice data known CAR effect. New shipped areal example nc_sids (North Carolina SIDS, 100 counties) queen contiguity weights nc_sids_W, built offline (CSV/.rda-decoupled) sf. Imports spdep, Matrix, methods (sparse CAR precision/solver); sf Suggests (polygon input).

title: spmixqr 0.1.0
text: First release. spmixqr() fits spatial finite mixtures quantile regressions: latent regimes spatially varying mixing probabilities (softmax spatial basis) quantile-regression components whose covariate slopes spatial surfaces (scalar per-regime intercepts). G = 1 penalised spatially-varying-coefficient quantile regression lineage Reich, Fuentes & Dunson (2011). Penalised EM reusing mixqr component machinery (weighted_rq, constrained_kde) vendored, spatially penalised multinomial-logit gate. spatially-varying-slope component step uses convolution-smoothed check loss (Fernandes, Guerre & Horta 2021) bandwidth annealing, roughness penalty applies; reduces exact weighted quantile regression slopes flat. Spatial bases (thin-plate, low-rank Gaussian process, Markov random field) roughness penalties built mgcv; coordinates standardised internally transform stored prediction new locations. Classification-aware inference: fast classification-conditional sandwich, recommended spatial-block / xy-pairs bootstrap cross-replicate label alignment. Identifiability labels: free spatial intercept surface separately identified gate, spatial level carried gate; components ordered sign-stable separating slope, label_stability diagnostic warns slope surfaces cross. spmixqr_select() (BIC cross-validated check loss), full S3 method surface, coef_surface() / gate_surface() accessors, sim_spmixqr() generator, built-meuse_zinc dataset. Validation: recovery held-locations, Monte-Carlo coverage, two negative controls (-flat aliasing-targeted), reductions quantreg, mixqr, mixqrgate (see inst/benchmarks inst/replication).
